She is the daughter of the god Takagimiketsunohakatatenojihime, and the mother of two other gods, Amaterasu and Takamimusuhiko.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakui...'
revision.text:
Takuhadachiji-hime, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god Takagimiketsunohakatatenojihime, and the mother of two other gods, Amaterasu and Takamimusuhiko.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name=":0" />
In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name=":0" />
In medieval times, she was associated with the Buddhist figure of the sixth heaven devil king and was worshiped in certain sects of Buddhism. The historical figure Oda Nobunaga is said to have held this deity in high esteem and even identified himself as its incarnation. With the separation of Buddhism and Shinto in the Meiji period, many temples dedicated to this deity were converted into Shinto shrines and their names were changed.<ref name=":0" />
== References ==

Takuhadachiji-hime, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god Takagimiketsunohakatatenojihime, and the mother of two other gods, Amaterasu and Takamimusuhiko.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], and thus an ancestor of [[Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>
The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name=":0" />
In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name=":0" />
In medieval times, she was associated with the Buddhist figure of the sixth heaven devil king and was worshiped in certain sects of Buddhism. The historical figure Oda Nobunaga is said to have held this deity in high esteem and even identified himself as its incarnation. With the separation of Buddhism and Shinto in the Meiji period, many temples dedicated to this deity were converted into Shinto shrines and their names were changed.<ref name=":0" />
== References ==

Takuhadachiji-hime, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<!-- and the mother of two other gods, Amaterasu and Takamimusuhiko -->.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], and thus an ancestor of [[Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>
The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name=":0" />
In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name=":0" />
In medieval times, she was associated with the Buddhist figure of the sixth heaven devil king and was worshiped in certain sects of Buddhism. The historical figure Oda Nobunaga is said to have held this deity in high esteem and even identified himself as its incarnation. With the separation of Buddhism and Shinto in the Meiji period, many temples dedicated to this deity were converted into Shinto shrines and their names were changed.<ref name=":0" />
== References ==

She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<!-- and the mother of two other gods, Amaterasu and Takamimusuhiko -->.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":1" />, and thus an ancestor of [[Jimmu]].<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Takuhadachiji-hime'''|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>

The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

{{nihongo|'''Takuhadachiji-hime'''|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>

The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>

The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

{{nihongo|'''Takuhadachiji-hime'''|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>

The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yorozuhatahime]][[en:Takuhadachiji-hime]][[ja:栲幡千千姫命]][[ru:Ёродзухататоёакицуси-химэ-но микото]][[zh:栲幡千千姬命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yorozuhatahime]][[en:Takuhadachiji-hime]][[ja:栲幡千千姫命]][[ru:Ёродзухататоёакицуси-химэ-но микото]][[zh:栲幡千千姬命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yorozuhatahime]][[en:Takuhadachiji-hime]][[ja:栲幡千千姫命]][[ru:Ёродзухататоёакицуси-химэ-но микото]][[zh:栲幡千千姬命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11110848]] Wikidata name: Takuhata Chijihime no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese deity}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | consort ={{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Oshihomimi|WD=Q462555}}| children = [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], [[Amenohoakari]] | father = [[Takamimusubi]] | siblings = [[Omoikane|Omoikane]] | image = File:Kakuhatachijihime shinbutsu-zue.jpg | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | parents = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | offspring = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|WD=Q91088537}}; {{ill|Nigihayahi|WD=Q10949054}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}}; {{ill|honoakari|WD=Q10940411}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Takuhadachiji-hime'''|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>
The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== Genealogy == {{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Takuhadachiji-hime'''|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>
The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

{{Short description|Japanese deity}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | consort ={{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; {{ill|Oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}| children = [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], [[Amenohoakari]] | father = [[Takamimusubi]] | siblings = [[Omoikane|Omoikane]] | image = File:Kakuhatachijihime shinbutsu-zue.jpg | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}} | parents = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} | offspring = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|WD=Q91088537|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}; {{ill|Nigihayahi|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|honoakari|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Takuhadachiji-hime'''|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, is a deity that appears in the creation story of the "Kojiki" and "Nihon Shoki." She is the daughter of the god [[Takamimusubi]]<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=万幡豊秋津師比売命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業 |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yorozuhatatoyoakitsushihimenomikoto/ |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="https-9716a" /> and younger sister of [[Omoikane|Omoikane]].<ref name="https-9716a">https://archive.today/20230406174104/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9716</ref> She is the goddess of textiles and mother of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amenohoakari]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=タクハタチヂヒメ |url=https://nihonsinwa.com/page/155.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=nihonsinwa.com |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ninigi |url=https://mythopedia.com/topics/ninigi |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=Mythopedia |language=en}}</ref> and thus an ancestor of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref name="xnu9ju32nb2az79bteaasia-2023a">{{Cite web |title=栲幡千千姫命（たくはたちぢひめのみこと）ご利益と神社 |url=https://xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia/shinto14/shrine88.html |access-date=2023-01-17 |website=xn--u9ju32nb2az79btea.asia |language=ja}}</ref>
The meaning of her name is not entirely clear, but "Takuhadachiji-hime" is believed to mean "many woven fabrics" or "many beautiful fabrics." There are also theories that the name refers to the deity as a skilled weaver or that it relates to the island of Toyoakitsu.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
In the creation story, Takuhadachiji-hime is said to be a part of the pantheon of gods who descended from the heavens to create the world. The different texts give different genealogies and roles to the deity, but she is consistently represented as a divine being associated with creation and fertility.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
